notes: Thickness measurements at grid positions across top and back plates. Arch heights are separate from plate thickness.

back.arch_height_source: 3/4 inch, highest point just below waist (per sketch annotation)

observations: Top and back share similar proportional gradient — thin at edges (~3mm), rising through mid-zones (~3.5-4mm), heaviest under bridge zone (~4-4.5mm).
Back arch is higher than top (3/4 vs 5/8 inch) — consistent with traditional archtop practice where back provides structural dome.
Back has thinner readings in upper bout (2.0-2.75mm) vs top — back upper bout is acoustically less critical.
Proportional shape is consistent with Benedetto graduation principles — this confirms normalized template approach is valid across makers.
These absolute values are one maker's solution for one piece of wood. The normalized ratios are the transferable knowledge.
